sentences.text: Jeg vil jo bare være her
sentences.translation: I just want to be here

words.grammar: bare · very common filler word in Danish

sentences.text: Hvor himlen møder havet

sentences.text: Og alt er stille nu
sentences.translation: And everything is quiet now

words.grammar: stille · same form in all positions

sentences.text: Og natten er så smuk
sentences.translation: And the night is so beautiful

sentences.text: Jeg lukker mine øjne
sentences.translation: I close my eyes

et øje (an eye) · øjne (eyes) · irregular plural

sentences.text: Og lytter til din stemme
sentences.translation: And listen to your voice

words.grammar: at lytte (to listen) · lytte til = listen to

lyrics.title: Det' kun vigtigt, hvad det er

sentences.text: Fuck det, nu vælter solen ind
sentences.translation: Screw it, now the sun is pouring in

sentences.text: Og fortryller alle støvkorn på min bordplade
sentences.translation: And enchanting every dust particle on my tabletop

sentences.text: Det' helt okay at være til
sentences.translation: It's totally okay to exist

sentences.text: Luften sitrer, når jeg rører ved din overflade
sentences.translation: The air trembles when I touch your surface

sentences.text: Det' weird at tænk' på, du' en anden, så jeg prøver at la' være
sentences.translation: It's weird to think about, you're with someone else, so I'm trying to hold back

sentences.text: Men du' ligeglad med, hva' det ligner - det' kun vigtigt, hva' det er
sentences.translation: But you don't care what it looks like — it only matters what it is

sentences.text: Fuck det, nu lukker jeg dig ind
sentences.translation: Screw it, now I'm letting you in

sentences.text: For jeg smelter af den måde, du si'r hej på
sentences.translation: Because I melt at the way you say hello

sentences.text: Det' nok til, jeg ringer op igen
sentences.translation: That's enough for me to call again

sentences.text: Foruden 22 måder, du er dig på
sentences.translation: Without the 22 ways you are yourself

sentences.text: Det' weird at tænk' på, du' en anden, så jeg prøver at la' være
sentences.translation: It's weird to think about, you're with someone else, so I'm trying to hold back

sentences.text: Men du' ligeglad med, hva' det ligner - det' kun vigtigt, hva' det er
sentences.translation: But you don't care what it looks like — it only matters what it is

sentences.text: Hold mig tæt til dit hoved, hold dit vejr
sentences.translation: Hold me close to your head, hold your breath

sentences.text: Før du griber mig med øjnene og dit mod
sentences.translation: Before you catch me with your eyes and your courage

sentences.text: Før jeg møder dig der
sentences.translation: Before I meet you there

sentences.text: Hvor jeg næsten ikke fatter ting med mit hoved
sentences.translation: Where I can barely understand things with my mind

words.definition: understands / grasps (colloquial: jeg fatter det ikke = I don't get it)

sentences.text: Det roder, men modet ka' opløse rodet
sentences.translation: It's a mess, but courage can dissolve the mess

words.definition: to be messy / muddled (det roder = it's a mess)

sentences.text: Det' weird at tænk' på, du' en anden, så jeg prøver at la' være
sentences.translation: It's weird to think about, you're with someone else, so I'm trying to hold back

sentences.text: Og det' weird at tænk' på, at det faktisk ikk' behøver være svært, nej
sentences.translation: And it's weird to think about, that it actually doesn't have to be hard, no

sentences.text: Og det' weird at tænk' på, at jeg nærmest kun har kendt dig et kvarter
sentences.translation: And it's weird to think about, that I've barely known you a quarter of an hour

words.definition: a quarter of an hour / a neighbourhood

sentences.text: For du' ligeglad med, hva' det ligner - det' kun vigtigt, hva' det er
sentences.translation: Because you don't care what it looks like — it only matters what it is

metadata.titleEn: It Only Matters What It Is

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Det' kun vigtigt, hva' det er.
grammarPoints.explanation: Guldimund's lyrics use heavy spoken contractions: 'det'' = 'det er', 'du'' = 'du er', 'hva'' = 'hvad', 'tænk'' = 'tænke', 'la'' = 'lade', 'ikk'' = 'ikke'. These dropped endings are typical of casual Danish speech and very common in songs and podcasts.

grammarPoints.rule: 'Prøve at' + infinitive (to try to do something)
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Prøve at' + infinitive means 'to try to do something'. 'La' være' = 'lade være' = 'to refrain / stop'. The infinitive marker 'at' always appears between 'prøver' and the following verb.

grammarPoints.rule: 'Være til' — to exist / to be alive
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Det' helt okay at være til.
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Være til' literally means 'to be present/here' but colloquially means 'to exist' or 'to be in the world'. 'Det er okay at være til' = 'It is okay to exist.' A gentle and very common Danish expression.

sentences.text: Åh, jeg ku' smadre noget, lige nu og her
sentences.translation: Oh, I could smash something, right here and now

sentences.text: Åh, eller ta' afsted

sentences.text: Hey, hey, ja dig

sentences.translation: Do you want something? Do you want something with me?

sentences.text: Her ligger Martin på en mark, de siger han har fået angst
sentences.translation: Here Martin lies in a field, they say he's developed anxiety

sentences.text: Prøver nogle piller, drukner i afmagt, slikker sine ungdomssår
sentences.translation: Trying some pills, drowning in helplessness, licking his teenage wounds

sentences.text: Byen her keder ham, kom lad os smadre noget

words.definition: to bore / to be bored (keder = bores)

sentences.text: Hvor var hun henne i går?
sentences.translation: Where was she yesterday?

sentences.translation: He smiles, says everything is cool

sentences.translation: But they're whispering in the corners now

sentences.text: De siger hun var ham utro, en 2.g'er fra Bjerringbro
sentences.translation: They say she was unfaithful to him, a year-eleven girl from Bjerringbro

sentences.text: Åh, jeg ku' smadre noget
sentences.translation: Oh, I could smash something

sentences.translation: Right here and now

sentences.text: Åh, eller ta' afsted
sentences.translation: Oh, or just take off

sentences.text: Du og jeg
sentences.translation: You and me

sentences.text: Ud og finde nogen og snave med
sentences.translation: Go find someone to make out with

sentences.text: Vil du noget? Vil du noget med mig?
sentences.translation: Do you want something? Do you want something with me?

sentences.text: Jeg var kun 15, det var sejest bare at være ligeglad
sentences.translation: I was only 15, it was coolest to just not care

sentences.text: Alt hvad jeg vidste om intimitet, havde jeg set i et M!-blad
sentences.translation: Everything I knew about intimacy I'd seen in a teen magazine

sentences.text: Kom lad os smadre noget, eller finde nogen vi kan snave med
sentences.translation: Come on let's smash something, or find someone we can make out with

sentences.text: Og når jeg ser tilbage, alt det der var normalt
sentences.translation: And when I look back, all that was normal

sentences.text: Jeg drukner i afmagt og kedsomhed og smerte
sentences.translation: I'm drowning in helplessness and boredom and pain

sentences.text: Vil du noget? Vil du noget? Vil du noget med mig?
sentences.translation: Do you want something? Do you want something? Do you want something with me?

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Vil du noget? Vil du noget med mig?
grammarPoints.explanation: Literally 'Do you want something?' but 'Vil du noget med mig?' is a Danish idiom meaning 'Are you interested in me?' — colloquial way to express romantic intent. The ambiguity is intentional: it can mean anything from 'do you want to hang out' to 'do you like me'.

'Ku'' = 'kunne' (could), 'ta'' = 'tage'. At B2+ level, recognising these contractions is essential for listening comprehension in Danish — they appear constantly in songs, podcasts, and casual speech.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Drukner i afmagt og kedsomhed og smerte.
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Drukne' means 'to drown'. 'Drukne i noget' is a very common Danish metaphor: 'drukner i afmagt' = 'drowning in helplessness', 'drukner i arbejde' = 'drowning in work'. Use it whenever someone is completely overwhelmed.

sentences.text: Du har mødt en kvinde

sentences.text: Det var før, du mødte mig

sentences.text: Og det har skræmt dig

sentences.text: Nu er jeg helt ødelagt

sentences.text: For hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til

sentences.text: Hvis det var hende, du var gået med?

sentences.text: Sig mig, hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?

sentences.text: Jeg ka' kun tilbyde den her krop
sentences.translation: I can only offer this body

sentences.text: Er den tilstrækkelig?
sentences.translation: Is it enough?

sentences.text: For hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til?
sentences.translation: Because what could life not have become?

sentences.text: Hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?
sentences.translation: How long are you planning to keep wearing her necklace?

sentences.text: Sig mig, hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?
sentences.translation: Tell me, how long are you planning to keep wearing her necklace?

grammarPoints.rule: Conditional perfect: 'ku' være blevet til' (could have become)
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til?
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Ku'' = 'kunne', 'være blevet til' = past infinitive of 'blive til'. Together: 'could have become'. Full form: 'Hvad kunne livet ikke være blevet til?' = 'What could life not have become?' This expresses regret about paths not taken.

grammarPoints.rule: 'Gå med nogen' — to date / be in a relationship
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hvis det var hende, du var gået med?
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Gå med nogen' is a Danish idiom for being in a romantic relationship. 'Var gået med' = past perfect 'had gone out with'. 'Hvis det var hende, du var gået med' = 'If she had been the one you were with'.

grammarPoints.rule: 'Stille op' — to do / manage / offer a solution
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Stille op' here means 'what can I do?' — expressing helplessness. Very common: 'jeg ved ikke hvad jeg skal stille op' = 'I don't know what to do'. Context changes its meaning (can also mean to set something up or to compete in an election).

sentences.text: Med opmærksomheden rettet mod de nyspartlede huller i min stuevæg
sentences.translation: With my attention fixed on the freshly patched holes in my living room wall

sentences.text: Irettesætter jeg dig med et tonefald, så hårdt, at det ku' bryde is
sentences.translation: I scold you with a tone of voice so harsh it could break ice

sentences.text: Fordi du spilder noget suppe, jeg har varmet op
sentences.translation: Because you're spilling soup I warmed up

sentences.text: Jeg sætter tommeltot og langefinger mellem mine øjne
sentences.translation: I put my thumb and index finger between my eyes

sentences.text: Lukker dem i cirka ni sekunder, mens jeg ekshalerer dybt
sentences.translation: I close them for about nine seconds while I exhale deeply

sentences.text: Lægger hovedet på skrå og ser på dig, før jeg roder rundt i dit grydehår og smiler lidt
sentences.translation: I tilt my head and look at you, before I ruffle your messy hair and smile a little

sentences.text: Du rejser dig og danser lidt i stolen
sentences.translation: You get up and dance a little in the chair

sentences.text: Jeg siger, du ska' sæt' dig ned
sentences.translation: I say you have to sit back down

sentences.text: Du siger, jeg skal sige til mor
sentences.translation: You say I should tell mum

sentences.text: Du aldrig gider sove her igen
sentences.translation: You never want to sleep here again

sentences.text: Så spurgte du mig, hvordan Gud ser ud?
sentences.translation: Then you asked me what God looks like

sentences.text: Det er et virkelig godt spørgsmål, hva' tror du?
sentences.translation: That's a really good question, what do you think?

sentences.text: Du svared': »Jeg tror han bor i en landsby i Nordsjælland«
sentences.translation: You answered: "I think he lives in a village in North Zealand"

sentences.text: Jeg parkerer dig i brusenichen med en spand af plastik
sentences.translation: I park you in the shower with a plastic tub

sentences.text: Og legetøj fra BR, som vi har valgt sammen
sentences.translation: And toys from BR that we chose together

sentences.text: Og du synger: »na-na-na-na-na-na«
sentences.translation: And you sing: "na-na-na-na-na-na"

sentences.text: Jeg ka' høre dig, mens jeg rydder op
sentences.translation: I can hear you while I tidy up

sentences.text: Og vi griner, da jeg gi'r dig din pyjamas på
sentences.translation: And we laugh as I put your pyjamas on

sentences.text: Og jeg gnubber mildt, men energisk på din spinkle krop
sentences.translation: And I gently but vigorously rub your slight little body

sentences.text: Du rejser dig og danser lidt i sengen
sentences.translation: You get up and dance a little in the bed

sentences.text: Jeg siger, at du skal læg' dig ned
sentences.translation: I say you have to lie back down

sentences.text: Du siger, jeg skal sige til mor
sentences.translation: You say I should tell mum

sentences.text: Du aldrig gider sove her igen
sentences.translation: You never want to sleep here again

sentences.text: Så spurgte du mig, hvordan Gud ser ud
sentences.translation: Then you asked me what God looks like

sentences.text: Det er et virkelig godt spørgsmål, hva' tror du?
sentences.translation: That's a really good question, what do you think?

sentences.text: Du svared': »Jeg tror han bor i en landsby i Nordsjælland«
sentences.translation: You answered: "I think he lives in a village in North Zealand"

grammarPoints.rule: Reflexive verb 'rejse sig' (to stand up / get up)
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Du rejser dig og danser lidt i stolen.
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Rejse sig' = 'to get up'. 'Sig' is the reflexive pronoun. Other reflexive movement verbs: 'sætte sig' (sit down), 'lægge sig' (lie down), 'vende sig' (turn around). These describe movement of the body relative to itself.

grammarPoints.rule: Indirect question with 'hvordan' keeps normal word order
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Spurgte du mig, hvordan Gud ser ud?
grammarPoints.explanation: In Danish indirect questions, word order does NOT invert — subject comes before verb. 'Hvordan Gud ser ud' = subject 'Gud' + verb 'ser'. Compare: direct 'Hvordan ser Gud ud?' (inverted) vs indirect 'jeg spørger, hvordan Gud ser ud' (not inverted).

grammarPoints.rule: Separable verb 'se ud' (to look like / to appear)
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Se ud' splits: 'se' stays in verb position, 'ud' goes to the end. Meaning: 'to look like'. 'Hvordan ser han ud?' = 'What does he look like?'. 'Det ser godt ud' = 'That looks good'. One of the most frequent Danish phrasal verbs.

sentences.text: Du har åbnet dit hjerte, dit hjem og dit liv og din krop for mig
sentences.translation: You've opened your heart, your home and your life and your body to me

sentences.text: Du koger pasta og rister valnødder i honning og hvidløg, mens jeg finder en proptrækker
sentences.translation: You're boiling pasta and roasting walnuts in honey and garlic while I find a corkscrew

sentences.text: Du ligger på sengen i lyset uden top, jeg stopper op og tænker: hva' har vi gjort, vi skal ha' det så godt?
sentences.translation: You're lying on the bed in the light without a top, I stop and think: what have we done, we're going to have it so good?

sentences.text: Vi tog på roadtrip
sentences.translation: We went on a road trip

sentences.text: Til Møns Klint i din Ford Fiesta
sentences.translation: To Møns Klint in your Ford Fiesta

sentences.text: Fjorten dage efter jeg mødte dig
sentences.translation: Fourteen days after I met you

sentences.text: Jeg blev forført af
sentences.translation: I was captivated by

sentences.text: Den måde du holdt TED Talks om sexistiske strukturer på din arbejdsplads
sentences.translation: The way you gave TED Talks about sexist structures at your workplace

sentences.text: Først hang vi ud i et sommerhus
sentences.translation: First we hung out at a summer cottage

sentences.text: Med et par andre par, som vi ikk' rigtig kendte endnu
sentences.translation: With a couple of other couples we didn't really know yet

sentences.text: Men om natten var det kun dig og mig i en sejlbåd
sentences.translation: But at night it was just you and me on a sailboat

sentences.text: Der var stjerneklart, og intet sagt blev misforstået
sentences.translation: The sky was clear and starry, and nothing said was misunderstood

words.definition: starry and clear (of a night sky)

sentences.text: Og indtil videre er der ikke gået
sentences.translation: And so far not a single day has passed

sentences.text: En dag, hvor vi to ikk' har gennemgået
sentences.translation: Where the two of us haven't talked through

sentences.text: Alt, der flyver gennem hovedet
sentences.translation: Everything that flies through our heads

sentences.text: Du forærer mig din fremtid - det' ikk' nok for mig
sentences.translation: You're giving me your future — that's not enough for me

sentences.text: Ku' jeg blot ha' kendt dig altid?
sentences.translation: If only I could have known you always?

sentences.text: Det' som om, at hvert minut uden dig er spildt
sentences.translation: It's as if every minute without you is wasted

sentences.text: Nogen dage ser jeg os om tredive år i dine øjne
sentences.translation: Some days I can see us thirty years from now in your eyes

sentences.text: Du smiler til mig tværs gennem tid, der kommer
sentences.translation: You smile at me across through time yet to come

sentences.text: Når jeg ser dit navn på displayet
sentences.translation: When I see your name on the screen

sentences.text: Får jeg åndenød og rammes af en barnlig glæde
sentences.translation: I get breathless and am struck by a childlike joy

sentences.text: Vores liv er en lang samtale, som af og til bli'r ramt af, at vi af og til ska' sove
sentences.translation: Our life is a long conversation that occasionally gets interrupted by us occasionally needing to sleep

sentences.text: Så la' os tale noget mere om, hvordan vi vil male lejligheden
sentences.translation: So let's talk some more about how we want to paint the apartment

sentences.text: Og la' os tale meget mere om, hva' vores børn ska' hedde
sentences.translation: And let's talk much more about what our children will be called

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Jeg blev forført af den måde du holdt TED Talks.
grammarPoints.explanation: Danish passive: 'blive' + past participle. 'Jeg blev forført' = 'I was captivated'. The doer comes after 'af' (by): 'forført af den måde' = 'captivated by the way'. Also: 'rammes af en barnlig glæde' = 'is struck by a childlike joy'.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Nogen dage ser jeg os om tredive år i dine øjne.
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Om' with a future time expression means 'in X time from now'. 'Om tredive år' = 'in thirty years'. Compare: 'for tredive år siden' = 'thirty years ago'. The preposition signals direction in time: 'om' (forward) vs 'siden' (backward).

grammarPoints.rule: 'Af og til' — from time to time (adverb phrase)
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Vores liv er en lang samtale, som af og til bli'r ramt af...
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Af og til' means 'from time to time' or 'occasionally'. Sits in the middle field after the verb. Synonyms: 'somme tider', 'ind imellem'. Very common in everyday Danish.

sentences.text: »Hva' er det, der ikke er retfærdigt?« spørg' hun, mens hun læner sig ind over bordet

sentences.text: »Hør lig' her, jeg har rigtig mange sager, som er meget vær' end din«

sentences.text: »Det hjælper jo selvfølgelig ikk' dig, men din ordning er fin«

sentences.text: »Det hjælper jo selvfølgelig ikk' dig, men din ordning er fin«
sentences.translation: "It obviously doesn't help you, but your arrangement is fine"

sentences.translation: Do you understand?

sentences.text: Hun si'r: »Jeg beklager, men din tid er gået«
sentences.translation: She says: "I'm sorry, but your time is up"

sentences.text: Jeg drikker vand af en kaffekop
sentences.translation: I'm drinking water from a coffee cup

sentences.text: »You can't buy happiness, but you can buy coffee and that's damn close«, står der på den
sentences.translation: "You can't buy happiness, but you can buy coffee and that's damn close", it says on it

sentences.text: Hun si'r: »Jeg håber, du ku' bruge det til noget«
sentences.translation: She says: "I hope you found it useful"

sentences.text: Jeg drikker vand af en kaffekop
sentences.translation: I'm drinking water from a coffee cup

sentences.text: Hun si'r: »Hva' er det, der ikke er retfærdigt?«, mens hun læner sig ind over bordet
sentences.translation: She says: "What is it that's not fair?", leaning in over the table

sentences.text: »Du valgt' jo selv at gå«
sentences.translation: "You chose to leave yourself"

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Man vil højst sandsynligt vurdere, at barnet har størst tilknytning til moren.
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Man' = 'one' or 'people in general' — avoids naming a specific person. Very common in formal, legal, and bureaucratic Danish. 'Man vurderer' = 'it is assessed / one assesses'. This song uses bureaucratic register deliberately to convey the coldness of the system.

grammarPoints.rule: 'Sådan ligger landet' — that's how things are (idiom)
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Sådan ligger landet' literally means 'that's how the land lies' — an idiom for 'that's the situation / that's how it is'. Used to bluntly dismiss or explain a reality. Very common: 'Sådan er det bare, sådan ligger landet'.

grammarPoints.rule: Reported speech with 'hun si'r:' (direct quote with colon)
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hun si'r: »Jeg beklager, men din tid er gået«
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Si'r' = 'siger' (says) — contracted. Danish introduces direct speech with a colon, then the words in » « quotes. This is the standard Danish dialogue format in writing. The » « marks are used where English would use double quotes.
